message:
Fetch contributions from GitHub + fix OAuth login loop (#10107)

* ✨ Fetch recent contributions from GitHub Search API for Contribute modal

The Contribute modal's Updates tab showed "No contributions found" because
the leaderboard JSON (used by the Netlify function) doesn't include individual
items. Now the frontend fetches the last 20 issues/PRs via the Go backend's
GitHub proxy (/api/github/search/issues) using the server-side GitHub token,
and displays them alongside the existing rewards breakdown.

- Add /search/ to allowed GitHub proxy prefixes
- Fetch last 20 contributions in useGitHubRewards alongside rewards data
- Classify items as bug/feature/other issues or PRs opened/merged
- Add "View all contributions on GitHub" link with search URL

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🐛 Fix OAuth login loop caused by rate limiter starving /api/me

The apiLimiter (600/min) is shared across all /api/* routes. On startup,
the watchdog page and initial card burst consume the entire budget before
the user logs in. When /api/me returns 429 after the OAuth callback, the
frontend interprets it as "not authenticated" and redirects back to login.

Move /api/me and /api/me (PUT) out of the api group onto authLimiter
(10/min, separate bucket) so the user identity endpoint can't be starved
by dashboard card traffic.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🐛 Raise API rate limiter from 600 to 2000 req/min

Dashboard loads 30+ cards with SWR refresh, plus background health
polling and pod-issues streaming. The 600/min budget gets exhausted
within the first minute, causing 429s on legitimate user actions
like submitting feedback or viewing the profile.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🧪 Update useGitHubRewards tests for contributions fetch

The hook now makes 2 parallel fetches (rewards + contributions via
Promise.all). Update tests to expect 2 calls instead of 1, and find
the rewards call by URL pattern rather than assuming it's index 0.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* Update Go test: /search/ is now an allowed GitHub proxy path

The test expected /search/issues to be blocked, but it's now allowed
since we added /search/ to allowedGitHubPrefixes for the contributions
fetch feature.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -20,7 +20,7 @@ func TestIsAllowedGitHubPath(t *testing.T) {
 		// Blocked paths
 		{"gists", "/gists", false},
 		{"orgs", "/orgs/kubestellar", false},
-		{"search", "/search/issues", false},
+		{"search", "/search/issues", true},
 		{"empty", "/", false},
 		{"admin", "/admin/users", false},
 		{"events", "/events", false},
